title: Chester's Way
author: Kevin Henkes
description: Chester and Wilson have a set way of doing everything until Lilly moves into the neighborhood. Though different at first, they learn that new friendships can bring fun and flexibility.

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to Storyline Online, brought to you by the Screen Actors Guild Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到由美國影視演員協會基金會為您呈現的 Storyline Online。

vocabularyNotes.title: foundation
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「基金會」或非營利機構。

examples.english: The foundation supports educational programs.
examples.chinese: 該基金會支持教育計畫。

phraseNotes.title: brought to you by
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用於節目開場，表示「由……贊助/提供/呈現」。

examples.english: This event is brought to you by our local community.
examples.chinese: 本次活動是由我們在地社區呈現的。

sentences.englishOriginal: I'm Katie Leclerc.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是凱蒂·勒克萊爾 (Katie Leclerc)。

sentences.englishOriginal: And I'm Vanessa Marano.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是凡妮莎·馬拉諾 (Vanessa Marano)。

sentences.englishOriginal: Today I am using American Sign Language for the deaf and hard-of-hearing viewers,
sentences.chineseTranslation: 今天我將為聽障與聽力受損的觀眾使用美國手語，

vocabularyNotes.title: hard-of-hearing
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「聽力受損的、重聽的」。

examples.english: Subtitles help hard-of-hearing people enjoy movies.
examples.chinese: 字幕能幫助聽力受損的人欣賞電影。

sentences.englishOriginal: and we will be reading Chester's Way written and illustrated by Kevin Henkes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而我們今天將閱讀由凱文·漢克斯 (Kevin Henkes) 著繪的《切斯特的風格》 (Chester's Way)。

vocabularyNotes.title: illustrate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「為（書本、文章等）繪製插圖」。

examples.english: He has written and illustrated many picture books.
examples.chinese: 他寫作並繪製了許多繪本。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester had his own way of doing things.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特有他自己的一套做事方式。

phraseNotes.title: have one's own way of doing things
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，表示「有自己固定的行事風格或做法」。

examples.english: She has her own way of solving difficult problems.
examples.chinese: 她有她自己解決難題的一套方法。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Hello, my name is Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你好，我的名字叫切斯特。

sentences.englishOriginal: I like croquet and peanut butter and making my bed.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我喜歡槌球、花生醬，還有疊床單。」

vocabularyNotes.title: croquet
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「槌球運動」。

examples.english: They played croquet on the lawn on Sunday afternoon.
examples.chinese: 他們星期天下午在草坪上打槌球。

phraseNotes.title: make one's bed
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「整理床鋪、疊被子」。

examples.english: He always makes his bed right after waking up.
examples.chinese: 他總是起床後立刻整理床鋪。

sentences.englishOriginal: He always cut his sandwiches diagonally.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他總是把三明治對角切開。

vocabularyNotes.title: diagonally
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞，指「對角地、斜對角地」。

examples.english: Fold the paper diagonally to make a triangle.
examples.chinese: 將紙對角對折成三角形。

sentences.englishOriginal: He always got out of bed on the same side.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他總是从床的同一側下床。

phraseNotes.title: get out of bed
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「起床、下床」。

examples.english: It is hard to get out of bed on cold winter mornings.
examples.chinese: 在寒冷的冬天早晨，要下床真不容易。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he never left the house without double-knotting his shoes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且每次出門前，他一定會把鞋帶打雙結。

vocabularyNotes.title: double-knot
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「打雙結（防止鞋帶鬆脫）」。

examples.english: Make sure you double-knot your shoes before running.
examples.chinese: 跑步前務必把鞋带打雙結。

grammarNotes.title: Double Negative (never... without...)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「never... without + V-ing」為雙重否定句型，強調「每次做某事時，必定會……」。

examples.english: He never leaves home without taking his umbrella.
examples.chinese: 他每次出門一定會帶傘。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester always had the same thing for breakfast toast with jam and peanut butter.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特的早餐永遠吃一樣的東西：抹了果醬和花生醬的吐司。

phraseNotes.title: have... for breakfast
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，表示「早餐吃……」。

examples.english: I usually have cereal and milk for breakfast.
examples.chinese: 我早餐通常吃麥片加牛奶。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he always carried a miniature first-aid kit in his back pocket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且他的後口袋裡總是放著一個迷你急救包。

vocabularyNotes.title: miniature
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「微型的、縮小版的」。

examples.english: She collects miniature teapots.
examples.chinese: 她收集微型茶壺。

phraseNotes.title: first-aid kit
phraseNotes.explanation: 名詞片語，指「急救箱、急救包」。

examples.english: Every car should have a first-aid kit.
examples.chinese: 每輛車都應該配備急救包。

sentences.englishOriginal: Just in case.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 以防萬一。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: just in case
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為常用的口語表達，表示「以防萬一、以備不時之需」。

examples.english: Take an umbrella, just in case.
examples.chinese: 帶把傘吧，以防萬一。

sentences.englishOriginal: “You definitely have a mind of your own,” said Chester's mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你還真的很有自己的主見呢，」切斯特的媽媽說。

phraseNotes.title: have a mind of one's own
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「有自己的主見、非常有主見（有時亦指物品不聽使喚）」。

examples.english: Even as a toddler, she had a mind of her own.
examples.chinese: 即使還是個幼童，她就非常有自己的主見了。

sentences.englishOriginal: “That's one way to put it,” said Chester's father.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「也可以這麼說啦，」切斯特的爸爸說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: That's one way to put it
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語俚語，用來表示「這倒也是一種說法/看法（帶點微妙或委婉的保留態度）」。

examples.english: He's very unique. — Well, that's one way to put it.
examples.chinese: 他真的很獨特。— 嗯，也可以這麼說啦。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester's best friend Wilson was exactly the same way.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特最好的朋友威爾森也完全是這個樣子。

phraseNotes.title: the same way
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，表示「同樣地、也是這樣」。

examples.english: I feel the same way about this movie.
examples.chinese: 我對這部電影也有同感。

sentences.englishOriginal: That's why they were best friends.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這就是為什麼他們會成為最好的朋友。

grammarNotes.title: That's why + Clause
grammarNotes.explanation: 「That's why...」表示「這就是為什麼……」，後面接結果句。

examples.english: He practiced every day. That's why he won.
examples.chinese: 他每天練習，這就是為什麼他贏了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester wouldn't play baseball unless Wilson played, and they never swung at the first pitch or slid headfirst.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 除非威爾森一起打，否則切斯特絕不打棒球；而且他們從不揮擊第一球，也從不頭朝前滑壘。

vocabularyNotes.title: swing
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，在這裡指棒球中的「揮棒」。

examples.english: The batter swung at the fastball.
examples.chinese: 打擊手對著快速球揮棒。

vocabularyNotes.title: pitch
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，棒球用語指「投球、投出的球」。

examples.english: It was a perfect pitch for a home run.
examples.chinese: 那是個適合打全壘打的完美投球。

phraseNotes.title: slide headfirst
phraseNotes.explanation: 棒球術語，指「頭部朝前的頭部滑壘」。

examples.english: He slid headfirst into third base.
examples.chinese: 他頭朝前撲滑上了三壘。

grammarNotes.title: unless + Clause
grammarNotes.explanation: 「unless」意為「除非……否則不……」，引導條件子句。

examples.english: We won't go unless you come with us.
examples.chinese: 除非你跟我一起去，否則我們不去。

sentences.englishOriginal: Wilson wouldn't ride his bike unless Chester wanted to and they always used hand signals.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 除非切斯特也想騎，否則威爾森絕不騎自行車，而且他們騎車時總會比手勢。

phraseNotes.title: hand signals
phraseNotes.explanation: 名詞片語，指騎車或行車時用的「手勢信號」。

examples.english: Always use hand signals when turning on a bicycle.
examples.chinese: 騎單車轉彎時一定要打手勢。

sentences.englishOriginal: If Chester was hungry, Wilson was too, but they rarely ate between meals.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果切斯特餓了，威爾森也會餓，但他們很少在正餐之間吃零食。

vocabularyNotes.title: rarely
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞，指「很少、難得」。

examples.english: He rarely goes out at night.
examples.chinese: 他晚上很少出門。

phraseNotes.title: between meals
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「兩餐之間（吃點心或零食）」。

examples.english: Try not to snack between meals.
examples.chinese: 盡量不要在兩餐之間吃零食。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Some days I can't tell those two apart,” said Wilson's mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「有時候我真的分不清他們兩個誰是誰，」威爾森的媽媽說。

phraseNotes.title: tell someone/something apart
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，表示「分辨、區分（兩人或兩物）」。

examples.english: The twins look so similar that I can't tell them apart.
examples.chinese: 這對雙胞胎長得太像了，我根本分不出來。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Me either,” said Wilson's father.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我也是，」威爾森的爸爸說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Me either
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 日常口語中對否定句表達「我也不……」的道地說法（標準文法多用 Me neither 或 Neither do I）。

examples.english: I don't like horror movies. — Me either.
examples.chinese: 我不喜歡恐怖片。— 我也是。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson, Wilson and Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特和威爾森，威爾森和切斯特。

sentences.englishOriginal: That's the way it was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 事情向來就是這樣。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: That's the way it was
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 習慣用語，用來描述某種常態或以往既定的習慣情況，「以往一直都是如此」。

examples.english: They worked together for years. That's just the way it was.
examples.chinese: 他們合作許多年了，以往一直都是這樣。

sentences.englishOriginal: They loved to go on picnics.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們非常喜歡去野餐。

phraseNotes.title: go on a picnic
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「去野餐」。

examples.english: Let's go on a picnic this Saturday.
examples.chinese: 我們這週六去野餐吧。

sentences.englishOriginal: Once, when Wilson accidently swallowed a watermelon seed and cried because he was afraid that a watermelon plant would grow inside him, Chester swallowed one, too.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有一次，當威爾森不小心吞下西瓜籽、因為害怕肚子裡會長出西瓜幼苗而哭泣時，切斯特也吞了一顆。

vocabularyNotes.title: swallow
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「吞下、吞嚥」。

examples.english: Swallow the pill with a glass of water.
examples.chinese: 配一杯水把藥丸吞下去。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Don't worry,” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「別擔心，」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Now, if you grow a watermelon plant, I'll grow one, too.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「現在，如果你肚子裡長出西瓜，我也會長出一顆。」

grammarNotes.title: First Conditional (If + Present, Will + Verb)
grammarNotes.explanation: 條件句，表達將來可能發生的情況與結果。

examples.english: If you study hard, you will pass the test.
examples.chinese: 如果你努力學習，你就會通過考試。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester duplicated his Christmas list every year and gave a copy to Wilson because they always wanted the same things anyway.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特每年都會把自己的聖誕願望清單複製一份給威爾森，因為反正他們想要的東西總是完全一樣。

vocabularyNotes.title: duplicate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「複製、複印、製作副本」。

examples.english: Please duplicate this file for everyone.
examples.chinese: 請把這個檔案複製一份給每個人。

sentences.englishOriginal: For Halloween, they always dressed as things that went together salt and pepper shakers, two mittens on a string, ham and eggs.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 萬聖節時，他們總是扮成配套的東西：鹽罐與胡椒罐、一根繩子串著的兩隻手套，或是火腿配蛋。

vocabularyNotes.title: mitten
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「連指手套」。

examples.english: She wore wool mittens to keep her hands warm.
examples.chinese: 她戴著羊毛連指手套來保暖。

phraseNotes.title: dress as
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「打扮成……、裝扮成……」。

examples.english: The kids dressed as superheroes for the party.
examples.chinese: 孩子們裝扮成超級英雄參加派對。

phraseNotes.title: go together
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「相配、搭配得宜」。

examples.english: Peanut butter and jelly go together really well.
examples.chinese: 花生醬和果醬非常搭配。

sentences.englishOriginal: “They really are two peas in a pod,” said Chester's mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「他們兩個還真的是形影不離、一模一樣呢，」切斯特的媽媽說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: two peas in a pod
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為著名的英文成語，比喻兩人「極其相似、形影不離」（宛如同一個豆莢裡的兩顆豆子）。

examples.english: The two brothers are like two peas in a pod.
examples.chinese: 這兄弟倆簡直是一模一樣、形影不離。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Looks like it,” said Chester's father.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「看起來確實是這樣，」切斯特的爸爸說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Looks like it
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 常用道地表達，表示「看樣子是的、似乎確實如此」。

examples.english: Is it going to rain? — Looks like it.
examples.chinese: 要下雨了嗎？— 看樣子是的。

sentences.englishOriginal: In spring, Chester and Wilson shared the same umbrella.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 春天時，切斯特和威爾森共用同一把傘。

sentences.englishOriginal: In winter, they never threw snowballs at each other.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 冬天時，他們從不互相砸雪球。

phraseNotes.title: throw... at...
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「向……投擲……」。

examples.english: Don't throw rocks at the window.
examples.chinese: 不要拿石頭扔窗戶。

sentences.englishOriginal: In fall, they raked leaves together.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 秋天時，他們一起耙落葉。

vocabularyNotes.title: rake
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指用耙子「耙平、耙集（落葉或草）」。

examples.english: We raked the leaves into a big pile.
examples.chinese: 我們把落葉耙成了一大堆。

sentences.englishOriginal: And in summer, they reminded each other to wear sunscreen, so they wouldn't burn.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而在夏天，他們會互相提醒要擦防曬乳，這樣才不會曬傷。

vocabularyNotes.title: sunscreen
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「防曬乳、防曬霜」。

examples.english: Don't forget to put on sunscreen before going to the beach.
examples.chinese: 去海灘前別忘了擦防曬乳。

phraseNotes.title: remind someone to do something
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「提醒某人做某事」。

examples.english: Please remind me to call John tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 請提醒我明天打電話給約翰。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson, Wilson and Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特和威爾森，威爾森和切斯特。

sentences.englishOriginal: That's the way it was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 事情向來就是這樣。

sentences.englishOriginal: And then Lilly moved into the neighborhood.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後，莉莉 (Lilly) 搬到了這個社區。

vocabularyNotes.title: neighborhood
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「鄰近地區、社區」。

examples.english: A new family moved into our neighborhood.
examples.chinese: 一個新家庭搬到了我們的社區。

sentences.englishOriginal: I'm Lilly! I am the Queen!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是莉莉！我是女王！

sentences.englishOriginal: I like EVERYTHING!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我喜歡每一樣東西！

sentences.englishOriginal: Lilly had her own way of doing things...
sentences.chineseTranslation: 莉莉也有她自己一套獨特的做事方式……

sentences.englishOriginal: She wore band-aids all over her arms and legs, to look brave.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她的手手臂和雙腿上貼滿了 OK 繃，好讓自己看起來很勇敢。

vocabularyNotes.title: band-aid
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「OK繃、創可貼」。

examples.english: He put a band-aid on his scraped knee.
examples.chinese: 他在擦傷的膝蓋上貼了一塊 OK 繃。

phraseNotes.title: all over
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「到處、遍及」。

examples.english: There were toys all over the floor.
examples.chinese: 地板上到處都是玩具。

sentences.englishOriginal: She talked backwards to herself sometimes, so no one would know what she was saying.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她有時會對自己倒著說話，這樣就沒有人知道她在說什麼。

vocabularyNotes.title: backwards
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞，指「倒著地、向後地」。

examples.english: Can you count backwards from ten to one?
examples.chinese: 你能從十倒數到一嗎？

grammarNotes.title: so + Clause (purpose)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「so」引導目的子句，表示「以便……、這樣就可以……」。

examples.english: Speak louder so everyone can hear you.
examples.chinese: 大聲點說，這樣大家才能聽到你。

sentences.englishOriginal: I ma Yllil
sentences.chineseTranslation: 莉莉是我是（I am Lilly 的倒讀）

sentences.englishOriginal: And she never left the house without one of her nifty disguises.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且每次出門，她一定會戴上她酷炫的變裝道具之一。

vocabularyNotes.title: nifty
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，口語中指「極好的、巧妙的、酷炫的」。

examples.english: That's a nifty little tool you have there.
examples.chinese: 你那裡有個挺酷的小工具。

vocabularyNotes.title: disguise
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「偽裝、變裝用具」。

examples.english: He wore a wig as a disguise.
examples.chinese: 他戴著假髮作為偽裝。

sentences.englishOriginal: Lilly waved at all the cars that passed by, even if she didn't know who was in them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 莉莉會對所有路過的車輛揮手，即使她根本不知道車裡坐的是誰。

phraseNotes.title: pass by
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「路過、經過」。

examples.english: A red sports car just passed by.
examples.chinese: 一輛紅色的跑車剛好經過。

grammarNotes.title: even if (conjunction)
grammarNotes.explanation: 引導讓步條件子句，表示「即使、縱使」。

examples.english: I will go even if it rains.
examples.chinese: 即使下雨我也要去。

sentences.englishOriginal: And she always carried a loaded squirt gun in her back pocket.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且她的後口袋裡總是裝著一把裝滿水的滋水槍。

vocabularyNotes.title: squirt gun
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指玩具「水槍」。

examples.english: The kids had fun playing with squirt guns in the yard.
examples.chinese: 孩子們在院子裡玩水槍玩得不亦樂乎。

sentences.englishOriginal: Just in case.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 以防萬一。

sentences.englishOriginal: “She definitely has a mind of her own,” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「她還真的非常有自己的主見呢，」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “That's one way to put it,” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「也可以這麼說啦，」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: When Lilly asked Chester and Wilson to play, they said they were busy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當莉莉找切斯特和威爾森玩時，他們都說自己很忙。

sentences.englishOriginal: When she called them up on the phone, they disguised their voices and said they weren't home.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當她打電話給他們時，他們會壓低變聲，說自己不在家。

phraseNotes.title: call someone up
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「給某人打電話」。

examples.english: Call me up when you arrive at the station.
examples.chinese: 你到達車站時給我打個電話。

sentences.englishOriginal: If Lilly was walking on one side of the street, Chester and Wilson crossed to the other and hid.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果莉莉走在街道的這一邊，切斯特和威爾森就會走到另一邊躲起來。

sentences.englishOriginal: “She's something else,” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「她真有夠特別的，」切斯特說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: someone/something is something else
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 道地口語，表示「某人/某事真是不簡單、太非同尋常了、真有夠獨特的」。

examples.english: Her new performance was really something else!
examples.chinese: 她這次的新表演真的很非同凡響！

sentences.englishOriginal: “Looks like it,” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「看起來確實是，」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: One day, while Chester and Wilson were practicing their hand signals, some older boys rode by, popping wheelies.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有一天，當切斯特和威爾森正在練習他們的手勢信號時，幾個年紀比較大的男生騎車經過，還特技翹前輪。

phraseNotes.title: pop a wheelie
phraseNotes.explanation: 俚語片語，指騎單車或機車時「特技拉起前輪、翹前輪」。

examples.english: He showed off by popping a wheelie in front of his friends.
examples.chinese: 他在朋友面前表演特技翹前輪炫耀。

sentences.englishOriginal: They circled Chester and Wilson and yelled personal remarks.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們圍著切斯特和威爾森繞圈圈，大聲喊著人身攻擊的話。

vocabularyNotes.title: circle
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「圍繞……盤旋/繞圈」。

examples.english: The birds circled in the sky above us.
examples.chinese: 鳥兒在我們上方的天空繞圈盤旋。

phraseNotes.title: personal remarks
phraseNotes.explanation: 名詞片語，指針對個人的「人身攻擊言論、不好聽的話」。

examples.english: It is rude to make personal remarks about others.
examples.chinese: 對他人發表人身攻擊的言論是很不禮貌的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson didn't know what to do.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特和威爾森不知所措。

sentences.englishOriginal: Just when they were about to give up hope, a fierce-looking cat with horrible fangs jumped out of the bushes and frightened the older boys away.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 就在他們準備放棄希望時，一隻看起來極其兇猛、長著可怕獠牙的大貓突然從灌木叢中跳出來，把那些大男生嚇跑了。

vocabularyNotes.title: fang
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指動物的「尖牙、獠牙」。

examples.english: The snake showed its sharp fangs.
examples.chinese: 那條蛇露出了它鋒利的獠牙。

phraseNotes.title: be about to
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「正要、即將（做某事）」。

examples.english: I was about to leave when the phone rang.
examples.chinese: 電話響起時我正準備離開。

phraseNotes.title: frighten away
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指「把……嚇跑」。

examples.english: The loud noise frightened the birds away.
examples.chinese: 巨大的噪音把鳥兒都嚇跑了。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Are you who I think you are?” Chester asked the cat.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你該不會就是我想的那個人吧？」切斯特問那隻貓。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Of course,” the cat replied.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「當然啦，」那隻貓回答。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Thank you, Lilly,” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「謝謝你，莉莉，」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “You're welcome, Chester,” said Lilly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「不客氣，切斯特，」莉莉說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Thank you, Lilly,” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「謝謝你，莉莉，」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “You're welcome, Wilson,” said Lilly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「不客氣，威爾森，」莉莉說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “I'm glad you were wearing a disguise,” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「真高興你有戴著變裝道具，」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “And I'm glad you had your squirt gun,” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「而且真高興你有帶水槍，」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “I always do,” said Lilly. “Just in case.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我一直都有帶啊，」莉莉說。「以防萬一嘛。」

sentences.englishOriginal: Afterward, Chester invited Lilly over for lunch.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那之後，切斯特邀請莉莉到家裡吃午餐。

phraseNotes.title: invite someone over
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「邀請某人到家裡做客」。

examples.english: We invited our new neighbors over for dinner.
examples.chinese: 我們邀請新鄰居到家裡吃晚飯。

sentences.englishOriginal: “You have a Muscle Mouse cup?!” said Lilly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你有『肌肉老鼠』的杯子？！」莉莉驚呼。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Of course,” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「當然囉，」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “I do, too!” said Lilly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我也有一個！」莉莉說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Same here,” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我也一樣有，」威爾森說。

grammarNotes.title: Same here
grammarNotes.explanation: 口語極常見表達，意為「我也是、我這邊也一樣」。

examples.english: I love iced coffee. — Same here!
examples.chinese: 我喜歡冰咖啡。— 我也是！

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson cut their sandwiches diagonally.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特和威爾森把三明治對角線切開。

sentences.englishOriginal: Lilly asked Chester's mother if she had cookie cutters and she made stars and flowers and bells.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 莉莉問切斯特的媽媽有沒有餅乾壓模，然後她把三明治切成了星星、花朵和鈴鐺的形狀。

vocabularyNotes.title: cookie cutter
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「餅乾模型、壓模」。

examples.english: We used star-shaped cookie cutters to make the biscuits.
examples.chinese: 我們用星形的餅乾模型來做餅乾。

sentences.englishOriginal: “That's neat!” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那太酷了！」切斯特說。

vocabularyNotes.title: neat
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，除了「整潔」之外，在口語中常代表「棒極了、酷的」。

examples.english: That's a neat idea!
examples.chinese: 那真是個棒透了的主意！

sentences.englishOriginal: “Wow!” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「哇！」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: That night, Lilly invited Chester and Wilson to sleep over.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那天晚上，莉莉邀請切斯特和威爾森到她家過夜。

phraseNotes.title: sleep over
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指在朋友家「過夜、參加睡衣派對」。

examples.english: Can my friend sleep over this weekend?
examples.chinese: 這個週末我朋友可以來我們家過夜嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: “You have a night light?!” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你有小夜燈？！」切斯特驚呼。

vocabularyNotes.title: night light
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「小夜燈」。

examples.english: The child sleeps with a night light on.
examples.chinese: 那孩子開著小夜燈睡覺。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Of course,” said Lilly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「當然囉，」莉莉說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “I do, too!” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我也有一個！」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Same here,” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我也一樣有，」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson wanted toast with jam and peanut butter for breakfast the next morning.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隔天早晨，切斯特和威爾森早餐想吃抹了果醬和花生醬的吐司。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Boring,” said Lilly. “Try this instead.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「無聊，」莉莉說。「改吃吃看這個吧。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “This is good!” said Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這真好吃！」切斯特說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Wow!” said Wilson.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「哇！」威爾森說。

sentences.englishOriginal: After that, when Lilly asked Chester and Wilson to play, they said yes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那之後，當莉莉找切斯特和威爾森玩時，他們都一口答應。

sentences.englishOriginal: When she called them up on the phone, they had pleasant conversations.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當她打電話給他們時，他們聊得很愉快。

vocabularyNotes.title: pleasant
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「令人愉快的、宜人的」。

examples.english: We had a very pleasant walk in the park.
examples.chinese: 我們在公園裡散步散得很愉快。

sentences.englishOriginal: And if Lilly was walking on one side of the street, Chester and Wilson waved and ran to catch up with her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果莉莉走在街道的一邊，切斯特和威爾森會揮揮手，然後小跑過去趕上她。

phraseNotes.title: catch up with
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「追上、趕上（某人）」。

examples.english: Go ahead, I will catch up with you later.
examples.chinese: 你先走，我等一下會趕上你。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson taught Lilly hand signals.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特和威爾森教莉莉打手勢信號。

sentences.englishOriginal: And she taught them how to pop wheelies.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而她則教他們怎麼特技翹前輪。

sentences.englishOriginal: Lilly taught Chester and Wilson how to talk backwards
sentences.chineseTranslation: 莉莉教切斯特和威爾森怎麼倒著說話

sentences.englishOriginal: “I Ma Nosliw”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「森爾威是我是」（I am Wilson 的倒讀）

sentences.englishOriginal: Olleh
sentences.chineseTranslation: 好你（Hello 的倒讀）

sentences.englishOriginal: And they taught her how to double-knot her shoes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而他們教她怎麼把鞋帶打雙結。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Some days I can't tell those three apart,” said Lilly's mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「有時候我真的分不清他們三個誰是誰，」莉莉的媽媽說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Me either,” said Lilly's father.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我也是，」莉莉的爸爸說。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson and Lilly, Lilly and Wilson and Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特、威爾森和莉莉，莉莉、威爾森和切斯特。

sentences.englishOriginal: That's the way it was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 事情向來就是這樣。

sentences.englishOriginal: For Halloween, they dressed as The Three Blind Mice.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 萬聖節時，他們扮成「三隻瞎眼的老鼠」。

vocabularyNotes.title: Three Blind Mice
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 著名英文童謠與故事角色「三隻瞎眼的老鼠」。

examples.english: 'Three Blind Mice' is a classic nursery rhyme.
examples.chinese: 《三隻瞎眼的老鼠》是一首經典童韻。

sentences.englishOriginal: For Christmas, Lilly gave Chester and Wilson nifty disguises.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 聖誕節時，莉莉送給切斯特和威爾森酷炫的變裝道具。

sentences.englishOriginal: And they gave her a box full of multi-colored shoelaces-- extra long for double-knotting.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而他們送給她一整盒五顏六色的鞋帶——特別加長型，專門用來打雙結的。

vocabularyNotes.title: shoelace
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「鞋帶」。

examples.english: Your shoelace is untied.
examples.chinese: 你的鞋帶鬆開了。

sentences.englishOriginal: They loved to go on picnics.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們非常喜歡去野餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: When Chester and Wilson told Lilly about how they had each swallowed a watermelon seed once, Lilly swallowed three of them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當切斯特和威爾森告訴莉莉他們以前各自不小心吞過一顆西瓜籽時，莉莉一口氣吞了三顆。

sentences.englishOriginal: “I'll grow a watermelon plant for each of us,” she said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我會為我們每個人都長出一株西瓜苗，」她說。

sentences.englishOriginal: In spring, Chester and Wilson and Lilly shared the same umbrella.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 春天時，切斯特、威爾森和莉莉共用同一把傘。

sentences.englishOriginal: In winter, they never threw snowballs at each other.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 冬天時，他們從不互相砸雪球。

sentences.englishOriginal: In fall, they raked leaves together.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 秋天時，他們一起耙落葉。

sentences.englishOriginal: And in summer, they reminded each other to wear sunscreen so they wouldn't burn.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而在夏天，他們會互相提醒要擦防曬乳，這樣才不會曬傷。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson and Lilly, Lilly and Wilson and Chester.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特、威爾森和莉莉，莉莉、威爾森和切斯特。

sentences.englishOriginal: That's the way it was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 事情向來就是這樣。

sentences.englishOriginal: And then Victor moved into the neighborhood...
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後，維克多 (Victor) 搬到了這個社區……

sentences.englishOriginal: So Vanessa, what did you think?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那麼凡妮莎，你覺得怎麼樣？

sentences.englishOriginal: I think that Victor, Lilly, Chester and Wilson are going to have a great time growing watermelons inside of themselves.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我覺得維克多、莉莉、切斯特和威爾森一定會很享受在自己肚子裡種西瓜的時光。

sentences.englishOriginal: I agree. I think the four of them will be good friends.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我同意。我覺得他們四個會成為好朋友的。
